title: Matter-Wave Interferometers as Open-System Dark Matter Detectors
abstract: Matter-wave interferometers (MWIs) offer a uniquely quantum route to dark matter (DM) detection: DM can reveal itself through phase and decoherence between spatially separated wavepackets, even when negligible energy deposition or resolvable recoil occurs. We formulate these effects in an open effective field theory for MWIs using the Schwinger-Keldysh formalism, which highlights a structural asymmetry between the two detection channels. For elastic spin-independent DM scattering, decoherence inherits novel Bose enhancement or Pauli blocking factors, while the phase is at most linear in the DM occupation number. By retaining the DM's coherence time, this framework spans Markovian and non-Markovian dynamics across a wide range of DM masses, and systematically organizes corrections beyond the heavy-probe limit.
summary: This paper establishes an open effective field theory framework to analyze how Matter-Wave Interferometers can detect Dark Matter by measuring phase shifts and decoherence. Using the Schwinger-Keldysh formalism, the authors show that the two observables respond differently to DM statistics, providing a powerful diagnostic tool for future quantum sensing experiments.
main_problem: To develop a theoretical framework for detecting Dark Matter (DM) using Matter-Wave Interferometers (MWIs) by analyzing phase shifts and decoherence induced by the DM environment.
main_result: The formalism reveals a structural asymmetry: decoherence inherits novel quantum statistical factors (Bose enhancement/Pauli blocking), while the phase shift is at most linear in the DM occupation number.
method: The problem is treated as an open quantum system, utilizing the Schwinger-Keldysh formalism to construct an effective field theory and calculate the reduced density matrix via an influence functional.
model_or_system: The system is an MWI (using atoms or mesoscopic objects) coupled to a background DM environment. The interaction is modeled via an effective Lagrangian describing monopole-monopole coupling.
key_observables: Phase shift and decoherence (contrast loss) of the matter wave.
important_parameters: DM mass, DM occupation number ($n_\chi$), coupling constant ($g$), and the ratio of interferometer time ($T$) to environment correlation time ($	au_c$).
main_assumptions: The DM halo is assumed to be stationary, homogeneous, and virialized, and the DM state is Gaussian.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the spacetime diagram for the MWI sequence, showing the unperturbed classical worldlines for the two arms.
paper_structure: The paper systematically derives the reduced density matrix using the path integral approach, expanding in powers of worldline fluctuations, and deriving the influence action to separate the phase and decoherence contributions.
why_interesting: This work directly applies advanced open quantum system techniques (SK formalism) to a physical platform (MWIs) relevant to AMO physics, providing quantitative predictions for environmental coupling effects.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. Spacetime diagram for the MWI sequence con- sidered in this work. The unperturbed classical worldlines XL,R(t) for a closed interferometric sequence geometry are shown in solid orange (right arm) and dashed green (left arm), together with the initial wavepacket of size σ (shaded tube).
